name: McCormick's Fire Direction Dugout
description: A reinforced earth-and-timber command post twelve feet below grade, smelling of cordite, damp wool, and kerosene lamp smoke. The map table dominates the center — a door laid across two ammunition crates, covered in oilskin and overlaid with the 1:10,000 grid map of the Cantigny salient.
atmosphericDetails: The kerosene lamp swings on its hook with each artillery concussion, throwing the shadow of the map table across the log walls in lurching arcs
A field telephone sits on a wooden shelf, its handset worn smooth by a hundred hands; the cord disappears into a rubber grommet in the earthen wall
The map-table case — a steel-banded oak box with a combination lock — sits open beside McCormick's chair, the fire tables inside visibly disturbed from their original order
